title: Varnish Ban and Purge Request Bursts
spl:
index=cache sourcetype="varnish:vsl"
| search "BAN" OR "PURGE"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count by _time
| where count > 50
description: Sudden ban/purge storms may follow bad deploys, cache poisoning response, or automation bugs.
value: Correlates cache invalidation with content management changes.
implementation: Ship filtered VSL lines to reduce volume; include username if management ACL logs it.
visualization: Timeline (events/min), table (operators), link to CMS releases.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: varnishlog forwarding (tagged VSL) or `varnishhist` pipeline.
‚ÄĘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cache` `sourcetype=varnish:vsl` or management log with `BAN` / `RP` tags.
‚ÄĘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
If VSL is unavailable, approximate via HTTP PURGE requests logged at the edge.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cache sourcetype="varnish:vsl"
| search "BAN" OR "PURGE"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count by _time
| where count > 50
```

Understanding this SPL

**Varnish Ban and Purge Request Bursts** ‚ÄĒ See the description and value fields in this use case JSON.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cache` `sourcetype=varnish:vsl` or management log with `BAN` / `RP` tags. **App/TA**: varnishlog forwarding (tagged VSL) or `varnishhist` pipeline. Rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` to match your deployment.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚ÄĘ Scope to the documented index and sourcetype, then apply transforms, thresholds, and `timechart`/`stats` as in the SPL above.

Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
Confirm events exist, field extractions match your format, and thresholds reflect production baselines.

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Add to a dashboard or alert; document ownership. Suggested visuals: Timeline (events/min), table (operators), link to CMS releases..
grandmaExplanation: We spot unusual mass invalidation of your cache so bad deploys do not silently flush content.
